fields.doc: Sealed frame format version. Single byte. Currently 2 (version 1 is the symmetric frame from contracts/encryption-frame/). Decoder MUST throw FrameVersionMismatchException on any other value. Bumping the version is a wire-breaking change that requires every consumer to handle both versions during the rollout window.

Byte count of the UTF-8 encoded recipient kid that follows. Single byte (max 255). Decoder validates against CONSTRAINT_MIN_KID_LENGTH / CONSTRAINT_MAX_KID_LENGTH — the same kid grammar as the symmetric frame.

UTF-8 bytes of the recipient key id — which recipient sealing keypair (active or retiring kid of the consumer service) opens this frame. Length is variable, declared by RECIPIENT_KID_LENGTH. The opener resolves the kid against its own private recipient keyring. The kid does NOT encode the recipient service id; service binding lives in the AEAD associated data and key derivation.

Byte count of the ephemeral public key that follows, as a 2-byte BIG-ENDIAN unsigned integer. A P-256 SubjectPublicKeyInfo is ~91 bytes — beyond a single-byte prefix. Decoder validates against CONSTRAINT_MAX_EPH_PUB_LENGTH before allocating. Offset is variable — comes immediately after RECIPIENT_KID.

The per-message ephemeral P-256 public key as SubjectPublicKeyInfo DER bytes. Raw binary (not UTF-8); length declared by the preceding EPH_PUB_LENGTH big-endian prefix. Wire-public by design — publishing the ephemeral public half is how ECDH-ES works; only the recipient's static private key can combine with it to derive the content-encryption key.

AES-GCM nonce. Fixed 12 bytes (GCM-spec). Offset is variable — comes immediately after EPH_PUB. Generated fresh per seal; the content-encryption key is itself fresh per message, so nonce reuse across messages is structurally impossible.

Ciphertext followed by the AES-GCM authentication tag. Length is variable (frame_size - VERSION - RECIPIENT_KID_LENGTH - RECIPIENT_KID - EPH_PUB_LENGTH - EPH_PUB - NONCE). The trailing CONSTRAINT_TAG_LENGTH bytes are the authentication tag; the decoder slices them off before decrypting the rest.
